archetype.name: Wrestler
archetype.note: This archetype offers Whirling Throw at a different level than displayed here.

feat.critical_failure: You don't throw the creature, and it's no longer grabbed or restrained by you.
feat.critical_success: You throw the creature any distance up to 10 feet, plus 5 feet × your Strength modifier. It takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier plus 1d6 per 10 feet you threw it. If you threw the target at least 10 feet and into a solid obstacle, use the maximum distance you could have thrown it to calculate the damage. The creature falls prone.
feat.name: Whirling Throw
feat.requirement: You have a creature grabbed or restrained.
feat.success: As critical success, but the creature doesn't fall prone.
feat.text: You propel your enemy away. Attempt an Athletics check against the foe's Fortitude DC. You take a –2 circumstance penalty to your check if the target is one size larger than you and a –4 circumstance penalty if it's larger than that. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your check if the target is one size smaller than you and a +4 circumstance bonus if it's smaller than that.

traits.name: Attack
traits.text: An ability with this trait involves an attack. For each attack you make beyond the first on your turn, you take a multiple attack penalty.

traits.name: Monk
traits.text: Abilities with this trait are from the monk class. A weapon with this trait is primarily used by monks.

sections.text: This feat can be used for one or more archetypes in addition to the listed classes. When selected this way, the feat does not have its class traits.
